meta.objective: maximize O = geomean_k G_k; G_k = geomean over in-sample shapes of tc_default_lat/seed_lat
meta.firewall: hill-climb on IN-SAMPLE only. VALIDATION read at adversarial checkpoints. TEST read ONCE at the very end.
meta.harness_certified: step1 harness-integrity: TritonBench unbiased (<1% vs hand-rolled, 1 kernel/call). commit bbd89997.
meta.gpu: CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=2 primary; GPU 3 free for parallel oracle sweeps. re-check nvidia-smi.

rms_norm_fwd.track: T1 (rollable rdim; reduction_loops has 1 entry; reduction_facts has 1 entry)

reduction_fact_observed.note: same across all in-sample shapes; only size_hint (rnumel) and m-block-floor vary

reference_observations.note: x vs eager speedup from step1 sanity (fp32, GPU2). G = tc_default_lat/seed_lat.

rms_norm_fwd.default_config_behavior: persistent (reduction_loops=[None]) for size_hint<=4096; looped chunk 4096 for size_hint>4096

sum.track: T1 (rollable rdim; 1 block_size, 1 reduction_loop, 1 reduction_fact, no matmul)

reduction_fact_observed.note: num_load=1 (reads x ONCE) vs rms_norm num_load=2 -- this is WHY sum is a wash (looped vs persistent both stream x once, equally bandwidth-bound) while rms_norm wins (looped re-streams x twice).

sum.default_config_behavior: same as rms_norm: persistent for rnumel<=4096, looped chunk 4096 above. Seed forces persistent up to 256KiB.
sum.status: WASH: G_seed=0.931 ~= G_default=0.933. Seed neither helps nor hurts (no regression). tc genuinely a bit faster for a 1-load sum.
sum.m_block_autotuner_min: 1 even at 32768 rows (rms_norm gets 2; the two-pass loads make rms_norm's grid-min trip differently).

long_sum.track: T1 (longsum naive + longsum_w_red_loop are rollable; longsum_manual is OUT-OF-SCOPE -- explicit hl.tile(n) inner loop, 2 block_sizes/0 reduction_loops/0 reduction_facts, 0 seeds)
long_sum.band: A (scalar-accumulator inner; tiny-M huge-rnumel -- looped + grid-occupancy stress test)
long_sum.rollable_target: longsum (naive) -- structurally identical to sum_kernel; what the tritonbench path uses
long_sum.default_config_behavior: looped chunk 4096 / warps 4 on these huge rows -> CATASTROPHIC (G_default 0.19-0.67). The un-seeded default is 3.3x slower than the seed.
long_sum.status: BIG WIN: G_seed=1.018 vs G_default=0.311 (3.3x). Seed reaches/slightly beats tc. The looped + grid-occupancy branches (previously untested) validated here.

layer_norm_fwd.track: T1 (rollable rdim; 1 block_size, 1 reduction_loop, 1 reduction_fact, no matmul). TWO reductions (mean=sum(x), var=sum(centered^2)) reduce over the SAME N rdim -> ONE rollable rdim -> 1 reduction_loop -> eligibility gate len(reduction_loops)==1 PASSES.
layer_norm_fwd.band: A (scalar-accumulator inner; two-pass like rms_norm but with num_reduction_ops=2)

reduction_fact_observed.note: num_reduction_ops=2 (two ReductionLowerings over the same N rdim: mean + variance). num_load=3 with bias (x+weight+bias), =2 without bias. num_store=3 (out, mean, rstd). All in-sample rnumel<=15872 -> persistent, warps ramp w4/w8/w16 (no shape crosses the w32 breakpoint 16384).

layer_norm_fwd.default_config_behavior: same as rms_norm: persistent for rnumel<=4096, looped chunk 4096 above. At wide rows (rnumel>=8192) the looped default LOSES big (re-streams x with poor reuse, like rms_norm num_load>=2). Seed forces persistent up to the 2^20 cap.
layer_norm_fwd.status: WIN: G_seed=0.989 vs G_default=0.886 (+11.7%). v4 champion handles it UNCHANGED (byte-identical heuristic; same persistent-workhorse + rnumel-warps-ramp mechanism as rms_norm). num_reduction_ops=2 does NOT want a different config (oracle keeps persistent + ties warps/stages).
layer_norm_fwd.tensor_op_warning: benign TensorOperationInWrapper on the host-side `if bias is not None` branch -- unrelated to the reduction seed; bind/seed/codegen all fine.

run_2026_05_29.what: Product B (time-first): does the registered seed make quick-autotune converge to a good config in LESS wall-clock? SEEDED vs UNSEEDED, N=3 random seeds {0,1,2} each, cold cache per run, full max_generations=5.
run_2026_05_29.heuristic: live registered TritonReductionHeuristic v4 (commit 37b27f67) -- the brief's champion-candidate seed
run_2026_05_29.env: H100/sm_90 fp32; GPU2 (rms_norm shapes) + GPU3 (long_sum,sum) for parallel timing, one autotune run per GPU
run_2026_05_29.autotuner: default LFBOTreeSearch, quick profile (init_pop=30, copies=2, max_gen=5, from_best_available, pad_random=False)
run_2026_05_29.knobs: HELION_AUTOTUNE_EFFORT=quick, HELION_FORCE_AUTOTUNE=1, HELION_AUTOTUNE_RANDOM_SEED in {0,1,2}, distinct HELION_AUTOTUNE_LOG per run; NOT HELION_SKIP_CACHE (would disable seed-from-cache)
run_2026_05_29.method_seeded: default: compiler_seed_configs n=1, autotuner_heuristics=['triton_reduction_tile']; seed enters gen0 of initial population. VERIFIED per run.
run_2026_05_29.method_unseeded: HELION_DISABLE_AUTOTUNER_HEURISTICS=1 -> compiler_seed_configs n=0, autotuner_heuristics=[]; gen0=default only. VERIFIED per run.
run_2026_05_29.seed_injection_verification: PROVEN per run via .driver.log: seeded n_seeds=1 + heuristic name present; unseeded n_seeds=0. Cross-checked against gen0 CSV (seeded gen0 = {default w4, seed w16/w32}; unseeded gen0 = {default w4}).

TRAP_seed_degraded_on_injection.finding: On ALL 4 shapes (rnumel>4096) the seed's reduction_loops=[None] (PERSISTENT) is silently flat-encoded to a LOOPED chunk of 4096 when injected into the autotuner. num_warps + block_sizes survive; the persistent lever does NOT.
TRAP_seed_degraded_on_injection.root_cause: ReductionLoopSpec._encode_flat_value (config_spec.py ~1799) maps None -> _flat_fragment.default() = min(next_pow2(rnumel),4096). unflatten restores None only if flat_int>=size_hint, false for rnumel>4096.
TRAP_seed_degraded_on_injection.proof: [None] codegen has no `for roffset` loop (1 tl.arange); [4096] codegen has the loop (2 tl.arange) -- different kernels. The Product-A bare-seed path (configs=[seed]) is UNAFFECTED (keeps [None]); only the autotuner-injection path degrades it.
TRAP_seed_degraded_on_injection.implication: Product-B wins are a LOWER BOUND -- the seed reaches gen0 carrying only its num_warps advantage, not its headline persistent lever. Fixing the ReductionLoopSpec round-trip should widen the early-budget gap. This is a registered-seed defect for the autotuner path; NOT a Product-A regression.
TRAP_seed_degraded_on_injection.open_lever: make ReductionLoopSpec round-trip None (sentinel that decodes back to persistent), or inject the persistent choice through a flat channel that survives. Worth doing before re-running Product B.

best_perf_vs_gen_median_ms._axis: best-perf-so-far vs generation index [g0,g1,g2,g3,g4,g5], median of 3 random seeds

slice1_same_budget_seeded_advantage._metric: unseeded_ms / seeded_ms (>1 => seeded faster) at budget, median of 3 seeds
slice1_same_budget_seeded_advantage.guardrail: PASS all 4: seeded >= unseeded at full budget (no regression). Sharpest at small budget as expected.

slice2_time_to_target_HEADLINE._metric: wall-clock (s) to first reach 95% of unseeded-full-budget perf; speedup=unseeded_t/seeded_t (>1 => seeded sooner). median[min,max] of 3 seeds.

long_sum_(8,131072).note: unseeded reached target 2/3; tiny-M ~10us noise floor

total_full_search_wallclock_median_s._caveat: the SEED makes the FULL 5-gen search LONGER (LFBO explores around the extra good config -> more neighbor configs compiled). Product-B value is TIME-TO-TARGET + early-budget perf, NOT a cheaper full search. The lever: with a seed you can SHRINK the budget (stop at gen1-2) and still land near the full-budget optimum.

run_2026_05_29.verdict: Seeding shifts the convergence curve UP-and-LEFT on all 4 shapes. Headline time-to-95% win 1.5-1.8x on 3/4 shapes (rms_norm x2, sum); long_sum ~tie on time-to-target but big gen0 win (3.8x at g0; absolute latencies ~10us noise floor). Full-budget no-regression holds. Achieved with the persistent lever DEGRADED on injection -- a lower bound. Honest: it is NOT a cheaper full search; the win is reaching a good config sooner / being able to shrink the budget.
run_2026_05_29.status: WORKER-PROPOSED; awaiting results-referee spot-repro of Slice-2 time-to-target on (2048,16384).

v1_triton_reduction_tile.anomaly_RESOLVED: (2048,2048): REFEREE CORRECTION -- NOT an artifact. Fresh-subprocess measurement is stable at G_seed~0.87 (tc genuinely faster at this shape: tc~17.6us vs both Helion variants ~20us). The seed (warps=8) mildly REGRESSES ~1.4% vs default (warps=4). The worker's 'isolated G~1.3' does NOT reproduce (worker error). Within -10% backstop but a real small loss, not a tie/win.
v1_triton_reduction_tile.all_shapes_codegen: persistent (every in-sample rnumel <= 16384 elems = PERSIST_MAX); looped branch UNTESTED on rms_norm

v2_triton_reduction_tile.measured: 2026-05-29 GPU2, kernel-only triton.testing.do_bench median-of-7, fp32 (WORKER; awaiting referee)
v2_triton_reduction_tile.tc_baseline: torch.compile default mode of the fp32 reference (rms_norm_pytorch; torch.sum(x,-1) for sum/long_sum)
v2_triton_reduction_tile.changes_vs_v1: PERSIST_MAX_BYTES 65536->262144 (64->256KiB); LOOPED_CHUNK 4096->16384; looped num_warps->32 (LOOPED_NUM_WARPS); NEW grid-occupancy branch (m_extent<GRID_OCCUPANCY_MIN=64 AND rnumel_bytes>=LOOPED_MIN_BYTES=131072 -> looped/warps32). rms_norm + sum byte-for-byte unaffected (their rows <=64KiB and M>=2048).
v2_triton_reduction_tile.O_note: NOT directly comparable to prior O=0.979 (rms_norm-only). Adding sum (a 0.93 wash) mechanically lowers a geomean even though the heuristic strictly IMPROVED (long_sum 0.31->1.02; rms_norm/sum unchanged). Honest claim is per-kernel: no kernel regresses, long_sum is a 3.3x generalization win.
v2_triton_reduction_tile.regression_check: rms_norm 0.982 vs champion 0.979 (no regression, within noise); sum no regression vs its default 0.933; long_sum no regression (default 0.311 -> 1.018). All within -10% backstop; none regress at all.
v2_triton_reduction_tile.correctness: PASS all shapes all 3 kernels (rtol=1e-3, atol=1e-3 for sum/long_sum -- atol=1e-3 to cover near-zero row-sum fp32 drift; allclose passes). Seed-used PASS (codegen persistent-vs-looped + num_warps match) -- evidence blocks in _lab/harness/evidence_new_kernels.py output.
v2_triton_reduction_tile.long_sum_codegen: (1,32768)/(2,65536) LOOPED via grid-occupancy branch; (4,130000)/(8,131072)/(16,262144) LOOPED via byte ceiling. All warps=32.

v3_triton_reduction_tile.measured: 2026-05-29 GPU2/GPU3, fp32 (WORKER; awaiting referee + auditor re-audit). rms_norm/sum median-of-7; long_sum fresh-process-per-shape median-of-9.
v3_triton_reduction_tile.tc_baseline: torch.compile default mode of the fp32 reference
v3_triton_reduction_tile.changes_vs_v2: DELETED byte-fence PERSIST_MAX_BYTES + grid-occupancy branch (both confounds). Persistent-vs-looped now keyed ONLY on the STRUCTURAL cap env.backend.max_tensor_numel (Triton 2**20 elems): persistent up to it, looped only above (where tl.arange over the row fails to compile). Moved num_warps=32 into the PERSISTENT path, gated on num_load==1 AND rnumel>16384 (STREAM_WARPS32_MIN_ELEMS) -- rms_norm (num_load=2) keeps the v1 ramp (never wants w32). _m_extent kept diagnostic-only.
v3_triton_reduction_tile.O_vs_v2: v2 O was 0.9763; v3 = 1.0064. long_sum geomean ROSE 1.018->1.099 (+8%), recovering the in-sample loss of the v2 looped branch. rms_norm 0.982->0.9815 (UNCHANGED, noise). sum 0.931->0.9449 (no regression).
v3_triton_reduction_tile.long_sum_codegen: ALL 5 in-sample shapes PERSISTENT/w32 (was LOOPED/w32 in v2). seed_used=True (codegen has no `for roffset`), correctness PASS (maxabs<=3.7e-4).

decisive_ab_seed_vs_p32._note: v3 seed IS persistent/w32, so seed/p32 should == 1.0 (a TIE -- that is the point: the v2 looped branch did NOT earn its place; persistent/w32 is the right config).

(1,32768).note: tiny-M 5us noise floor; byte-identical config; earlier 3-run gave 1.000

(2,65536).note: tiny-M 7us noise floor; byte-identical config; earlier gave 1.009

(16,262144).note: exact tie; one in-sample shape still <1.0, stages lever open

high_M_held_out_long_sum._note: lift above noise floor: persistent/w32 win generalizes across M; seed==p32 exactly at higher latency.

v3_triton_reduction_tile.looped_tail_disclosure: Looped branch fires ONLY for rnumel > 2**20 elems (Triton cap). NO in-sample shape reaches it. For (1,2097152): persistent/w32 FAILS to compile (numel 2097152 exceeds triton max 1048576) -> looped is STRUCTURALLY REQUIRED; v3 seed goes looped, correct, beats Helion default 10.8x (42.6 vs 462us) but loses to tc (G 0.303, tc uses multi-stage split reduction). Disclosed generalization tail, not tuned. _lab/harness/v3_looped_tail_check.py.
v3_triton_reduction_tile.regression_check: rms_norm 0.9815 vs champion 0.979 (+0.3%, no regression); sum 0.9449 vs its default 0.9365 (no regression, IDENTICAL warps to v2); long_sum 1.099 (no regression, +8% over v2). No active kernel regresses at all.
v3_triton_reduction_tile.correctness: PASS all shapes all 3 kernels (rtol=1e-3, atol=1e-3 for sum/long_sum near-zero row-sum fp32 drift). seed_used PASS (codegen persistent-vs-looped + num_warps match).

v4_widen_layer_norm.measured: 2026-05-29 GPU2, do_bench median-of-7, fp32 (WORKER; awaiting referee). Heuristic UNCHANGED (byte-identical v4 champion, commit c2845bdd; git 0 lines changed under helion/).
v4_widen_layer_norm.tc_baseline: torch.compile default mode of torch.nn.functional.layer_norm (fp32; with bias = tritonbench default)
v4_widen_layer_norm.change_vs_v4: NONE. Adds layer_norm_fwd to the active set. v4's persistent workhorse + rnumel warps ramp handle it directly (same mechanism as rms_norm). num_reduction_ops=2 + num_load=3 are DATA in the ReductionFact, not gated on.
v4_widen_layer_norm.all_shapes_codegen: persistent (every in-sample rnumel <= 15872 < 2^20 cap); warps ramp w4/w8/w16 (no in-sample shape crosses the w32 breakpoint 16384).
v4_widen_layer_norm.oracle_field_diff: quick-autotune + fair re-bench of FULL verbatim winner, 6-shape subset: G_seed=1.003 vs G_oracle=1.009 (~0.6%, noise). Oracle KEEPS persistent (reduction_loops=[None]) at the widest rows; where it picks w32 or a looped chunk it is perf-NEUTRAL vs the seed. Only real headroom: (4096,1024) small-N (oracle w2 + looped-256, ~4%) -- same tiny-N regime as rms_norm(32768,256)/sum, an unseeded indexing lever. => num_reduction_ops=2 does NOT shift the warps/stages optimum; no num_reduction_ops branch warranted.
v4_widen_layer_norm.no_regression_on_other_3: PROVEN byte-identical: git 0 lines changed under helion/; layer_norm_no_regression_proof.py = 27/27 in-sample seeds of rms_norm/sum/long_sum emit their exact v4 champion seeds. So G_rms_norm 0.980 / G_sum 0.937 / G_long_sum 1.10 are UNCHANGED (referee-confirmed for v4).
v4_widen_layer_norm.O_note: O_3kernel was 1.0034; adding layer_norm (G=0.989, a high-but-sub-1 kernel) mechanically nudges the geomean down ~0.4% even though the heuristic strictly improved layer_norm (+11.7% vs default). Same mechanical effect as adding sum. Honest claim is PER-KERNEL: no kernel regresses, layer_norm is a clean +11.7% win.
v4_widen_layer_norm.correctness: PASS all 11 shapes (rtol=1e-3, atol=1e-3; maxabs ~2-3e-6 vs F.layer_norm fp32). seed_used PASS (all persistent; codegen has no `for roffset`).

crossover_sweep_evidence.measured: 2026-05-29 GPU2, _lab/harness/crossover_sweep.py (rms_norm_fwd, best-persistent vs best-looped over warps{4,8,16}/stages{1,2,3}, median do_bench)
crossover_sweep_evidence.metric: pers/loop ratio (>1 => looped wins); rnumel in fp32 elems
crossover_sweep_evidence.verdict: persistent wins to ~256KiB for grid-occupied M>=1024 (loses from 98304/384KiB); to ~192KiB for grid-starved M=8 (loses from 65536/256KiB). Crossover ~64-256KiB, grid-occupancy-modulated. -> PERSIST_MAX_BYTES=262144 (occupied crossover).
crossover_sweep_evidence.looped_chunk_probe: _lab/harness/looped_chunk_probe.py: in looped region best chunk = 16384 (e.g. M=8/131072: chunk 2048->36.4us, 4096->25.1, 8192->23.5, 16384->22.0us); warps=32 best. -> LOOPED_CHUNK=16384, LOOPED_NUM_WARPS=32.
crossover_sweep_evidence.grid_occupancy_probe: _lab/harness/grid_occupancy_probe.py: persistent/16 vs looped(16384)/32 at rnumel 32768 & 65536 (both <256KiB), sweeping grid M: M=1..32 looped wins 5-21%; M>=64 wash. -> grid-starved (M<64) + rnumel>=128KiB forces looped/warps32. Threshold 64 ~= H100 SMs/2 (132).
crossover_sweep_evidence.v3_CORRECTION: The v2 crossover + grid-occupancy probes were a CONFOUND -- they compared persistent/w16 vs looped/w32 (warps NOT held equal), attributing a pure num_warps win to the loop flip. SUPERSEDED by v3_warps_held_equal below.

v3_crossover_sweep_evidence.measured: 2026-05-29 GPU2, _lab/harness/v3_crossover_sweep.py (sum_kernel num_load=1 = long_sum class; persistent vs looped BOTH at warps{16,32}; median-of-9 do_bench). The auditor's mandate: A/B with warps HELD EQUAL.
v3_crossover_sweep_evidence.metric: bestP/bestL over warps{16,32}; >1 => LOOPED wins; rnumel in fp32 elems

by_rnumel_across_M_1_4_16_64_256.524288(2048KiB): 1.20-1.22 @M<=16, ~1.0 @M>=64 (looped wins only at small M; NON-MONOTONE so NOT keyed -- 393216 & 786432 around it favor persistent)
by_rnumel_across_M_1_4_16_64_256.>1048576: persistent FAILS to compile (TRITON_MAX_TENSOR_NUMEL=2**20) -> LOOPED ONLY

v3_crossover_sweep_evidence.verdict: Persistent wins or ties at EVERY feasible byte size up to the 2**20-elem structural cap. There is NO perf-based byte fence below the cap. -> v3 persistent-vs-looped lever = the structural cap ONLY (env.backend.max_tensor_numel). The lone 524288/small-M looped win is non-monotone and NOT keyed.
v3_crossover_sweep_evidence.persist_warps_ramp: _lab/harness/v3_persist_warps_ramp.py (sum_kernel PERSISTENT path, best num_warps per rnumel): w8 @<=4096, w16 @8192, w32 from 16384 up (262144/M1: w4=47.9us->w32=11.1us = 4.3x). v3 sets w32 STRICTLY ABOVE 16384 so sum's max in-sample row (16384) stays w16 (unchanged).
v3_crossover_sweep_evidence.rms_norm_warps_ab: _lab/harness/v3_rmsnorm_warps_ab.py (rms_norm num_load=2): NEVER prefers w32 -- best is w4-w16 everywhere; at (32768,256) w16=574us, w32=1182us CATASTROPHIC (high warps couple badly with the small M-block at large-M/tiny-N). => the w32 win is a num_load==1 property, NOT generic huge-rnumel. v3 gates the w32 ramp on num_load==1; rms_norm keeps the v1 ramp.

oracle_cache._note: field-diff via _lab/harness/oracle_field_diff.py (Helion effort=full winner vs seed). Full-effort is SLOW (>10min/shape). NOTE the autotuner do_bench is NOT biased (harness-integrity proved <1% vs fair do_bench, 3 methods); the (32768,256) 'w32 artifact' was a FIELD-DIFF reconstruction error, see harness_integrity_correction. Prefer quick-effort + fair re-bench of the FULL verbatim winner (all levers).

(32768,256).harness_integrity_correction: NO autotuner bug. The 73.6us w32 result was NOT block=1 -- it was num_warps=32 paired with a LARGE M-block (block_sizes=[32]/[128], confirmed in autotune CSV). w32 is catastrophic ONLY at block=1 (1174us); at block>=8 w32 is ~36-41us. The field-diff mis-paired the winner's w32 with block=1 and re-benched that fabricated config. PROOF: (a) fair, autotuner do_bench, and interleaved_bench all agree <1% (block1/w32=1174us is REAL; 73.6us is a DIFFERENT config, also REAL); (b) full LFBO search tested block_sizes=[1] ZERO times (raise_grid_block_minimums floors M-block at 2 for 32768 rows); (c) the autotuner's OWN fastest-per-warps are all ~31-35us incl w32=34.3us, and it ranks LOW warps (w1=30.98us) best, NOT w32. See _lab/harness/autotuner_timing_bias.md. warps and block are COUPLED: never A/B warps with block fixed at 1. True fair optimum ~31us (low warps, block 2-4, looped reduction).
(32768,256).fair_rebench_caveat: [SUPERSEDED by harness_integrity_correction] oracle num_warps=32 is an ARTIFACT: fair end-to-end do_bench gives bs1/w32=1183us (G=0.029), bs1/w16=574us. FAIR optimum is LOW warps: bs1/w4=35.9us (G=0.950) ~= my seed bs2/w4=36.1us (G=0.945).
(32768,256).field_diff: persistent MATCHES; warps: oracle=32 (artifact) vs seed=4 (fair-optimal); block: oracle=1 vs seed=2 (floor), worth ~0.5% (noise). Seed at/near FAIR ceiling.

(32768,1024).field_diff: warps=4 fair-optimal (seed uses 4); high warps hurt. Seed at FAIR ceiling.

(2048,2048).REFEREE_CORRECTION: The isolated G~1.3 numbers below do NOT reproduce in fresh subprocesses. True stable value G_seed~0.87 (tc genuinely faster ~17.6us vs ~20us). Seed warps=8 mildly slower than default warps=4 (-1.4%). Open lever: warps=8 may be slightly wrong here vs warps=4; small, within backstop.
(2048,2048).field_diff: tc is genuinely faster at this medium shape; seed at warps=8 mildly regresses vs default warps=4. Minor; revisit num_warps breakpoints with coupled warps x block A/B.

(32768,256)_VERBATIM_winner.note: CONFIRMS harness-integrity: the verbatim winner is block=4/LOOPED-128/warps=1, NOT block=1/warps=32. The 'w32 artifact' was a field-diff reconstruction bug (fabricated block=1/w32). Field-diff verdict: oracle ~30.6us vs seed (persistent/block=2/warps=4) ~36us = ~15% headroom via larger M-block + small looped chunk + warps=1 at tiny N. tiny-N/large-M regime the seed under-serves.

sum._method: quick-autotune + fair re-bench of FULL verbatim winner (_lab/harness/oracle_reduction.py --kernel sum)
sum.field_diff: oracle ~= seed within ~1-3% at most shapes (both persistent for mid rows; (32768,256) oracle goes block>=2 + small looped chunk like rms_norm's tiny-N winner). Headroom is vs tc, not vs default -- via indexing/eviction levers not yet seeded. Low priority (sum is a wash).

long_sum._method: quick-autotune + fair re-bench of FULL verbatim winner (_lab/harness/oracle_reduction.py --kernel long_sum)

(4,130000).note: oracle PERSISTENT+stages3 -- our byte-ceiling sends it looped; 5% gap

(16,262144).note: same config, oracle re-bench faster -- noise in tiny-abs timing OR stages; investigate

long_sum.next_levers: num_stages>1 for the largest rows (oracle picks 3 on (4,130000)/(2,65536)); the chunk is near-optimal. NOTE tiny-absolute latencies (5-17us) are noisy run-to-run -- referee should pin fresh-process per-shape timing.
long_sum.v3_corroboration: The oracle's (4,130000) preference for [None] (PERSISTENT) -- flagged as a 5% gap our v2 byte-ceiling was missing -- is EXACTLY what v3 now does: all in-sample long_sum shapes are persistent. The oracle field-diff was an early signal of the v2 looped fence being wrong. Remaining oracle signal: num_stages=3 on the biggest rows -- now in the PERSISTENT path, an open lever (notebook open hypotheses).

iteration_history.change: Defined ReductionFact + populate in register_rollable_reductions; cloned CuteReductionTileHeuristic -> TritonReductionHeuristic (backend=triton); registered in HEURISTICS_BY_BACKEND['triton']. Persistent-vs-looped by rnumel-bytes; num_warps by rnumel; M-block at autotuner floor.
iteration_history.verdict: ACCEPTED. Referee-confirmed G_seed=0.9793 vs default 0.9083 (worker's 0.983 within noise). Auditor PASS. Champion. Next levers: raise PERSIST_MAX (fence) + strengthen looped branch; widen to more Band A kernels.

So the injected seed loses its persistent lever (keeps num_warps). Product-A bare-seed path UNAFFECTED. Product-B wins (1.5-1.8x time-to-target) are a LOWER BOUND. Fix pending blast-radius scoping.

v1_triton_reduction_tile.results_referee: ACCEPT. G_seed=0.9793 vs G_default=0.9083 (fresh subprocess/shape, N>=9 do_bench median). Correctness PASS all 13, seed used all 13. Worst per-shape -6.3% (32768,256). CORRECTION: (2048,2048) is NOT an artifact -- real G~0.87 (tc genuinely faster), seed mildly REGRESSES ~1.4% vs default there; worker's 'isolated G~1.3' does NOT reproduce.
v1_triton_reduction_tile.adversarial_auditor: PASS. No fabrication/wrong-thing/metric-gaming/autotune-smuggling. Overfit gap ~2.1% (held-out 0.962). DEFECT 1: PERSIST_MAX_BYTES=65536 is a FENCE at in-sample max; synthetic sweep shows persistent wins to ~65536 ELEMENTS (256KiB fp32) [16384,24576,32768,49152(1.356),65536(1.182) persist wins; 131072 looped wins]. Costs ~1.38x on held-out (1024,32768). DEFECT 2: (2048,2048) narrative wrong (small ~2-3% regression, not tie). NOTE: sum kernel is a wash (G_seed~=G_default~=0.94) -> rms_norm win is partly memory-pattern-specific; widen to validate generality.
v1_triton_reduction_tile.harness_integrity: NO autotuner timing bug (3 timing methods agree <1%). The w32 'anomaly' was a bug in OUR oracle_field_diff.py (flattened coupled multi-field winner, re-benched fabricated block=1/w32). warps x block are COUPLED. Oracle is trustworthy; field-diff must re-bench FULL verbatim configs. See _lab/harness/autotuner_timing_bias.md.

v2_triton_reduction_tile.decision: MECHANISM REJECTED (split gate). Referee ACCEPT on claims; auditor FAIL on mechanism (decisive). Per hub mandate, reject misattributed/effectively-smuggled results and iterate.
v2_triton_reduction_tile.results_referee: ACCEPT (claims-as-stated). long_sum G_seed=1.030 (worker 1.018, within tiny-latency noise) vs default 0.310 = 3.32x; persists at high-M (G 1.02-1.10) so not a noise artifact. sum wash 0.937 vs 0.929 (no regression). rms_norm 0.977 (no regression; threshold bump leaves it byte-for-byte persistent). Correctness honest (atol=1e-3 needed only for near-zero randn row-sums; tighter tol passes on well-conditioned inputs). Receipt _lab/harness/referee_v2_receipt.md. CAVEAT (referee's own): only A/B'd vs default, never vs persistent/w32.
v2_triton_reduction_tile.adversarial_auditor: FAIL (mechanism). Win is real but ENTIRELY from num_warps=32, not the looped/grid-occupancy branches. Controlled A/B holding warps EQUAL (AUDIT_seed_vs_p32.py): persistent/w32 BEATS shipped looped/w32 on 8/9 long_sum shapes -- (4,130000) 1.21x, (4,262143 held) 2.65x, (1,100000 held) 1.30x, (8,131072) 1.04x, (16,262144) 1.02x, (32,65536 held grid-occ branch) 1.02x (~0 benefit at its target shape); only (1,1048576 held, 4MiB) wants looped (0.76x). grid-occupancy premise is a CONFOUND (worker A/B'd persistent/w16 vs looped/w32). Branches net-harmful + carve out long_sum's shapes => kernel-identity smuggling in effect. FIX: seed persistent+warps32 for tiny-M huge-row; looped only for >~1MiB rows; delete grid-occupancy branch or re-key on warps; extend num_warps ramp to 32 in PERSISTENT path; raise looped byte-ceiling far above 256KiB; A/B vs persistent/w32 NOT vs default. Honest parts confirmed: no literal kernel-id branch, correctness honest, no regression, num_load wash explanation sound.

v3_triton_reduction_tile.decision: SPLIT (auditor wins): num_load GATE REJECTED -> surgical fix in v4. Referee ACCEPT (in-sample O~1.005 reproduces; v3==persistent/w32). Auditor FAIL: the `num_load==1` condition in the w32 gate is (a) INERT in-sample (0/27 shapes change -- no num_load>=2 shape has rnumel>16384), (b) FALSE physics (matched-pair A/B: w32 benefit is driven by RNUMEL not num_load; num_load=2 ALSO wants w32 at large rnumel -- 131072 nl2 w32/w16=0.57), (c) HARMFUL out-of-sample (real rms_norm (1,131072) w32/w16=0.725 wants w32 but gate denies; layer_norm nl=3 (1,131072) gate's w16 is 34% slower than w32). It's a curriculum-split fence dressed as physics. ACCEPTED parts of v3 (persistent workhorse, rnumel-based w32 ramp, structural looped tail, grid-occ + byte-fence DELETION) stand. FIX (v4): drop num_load from the w32 gate -> key on rnumel alone (byte-identical in-sample, recovers 30-40% on held-out large-rnumel multi-load).
v3_triton_reduction_tile.results_referee: ACCEPT. long_sum geomean G_seed~1.10 (two runs 1.0988/1.1135) vs v2 1.018 (+8%); high-M trustworthy: (256,131072)=1.040, (256,262144)=1.093, all seed/p32~1.000 (v3 IS persistent/w32; branches don't lose to the simple alt). rms_norm 0.9803 (+0.1% vs champ, no regression); sum 0.9363-0.9373 (no regression). O=1.003-1.0075 (avg 1.0052), >1.0. Correctness PASS (maxabs<=4.9e-4). seed-used proven at codegen level. Tiny-M long_sum per-shape is noisy (5-17us); geomean + high-M are the trustworthy evidence.
v3_triton_reduction_tile.adversarial_auditor: FAIL (num_load fence). PASSED: grid-occ fence GONE (all in-sample persistent, no M-carve-out), looped tail structural-only + honest ((1,2097152) persistent fails to compile, looped correct), long_sum win real+correct+==persistent/w32, no in-sample regression, correctness honest. FAILED: num_load==1 gate is inert in-sample (0/27 via AUDITOR_gate_inert_proof.py), false-physics (AUDITOR_numload_warps_ab.py matched-pair: w32 keyed on rnumel for BOTH num_load), harmful OOS (AUDITOR_rmsnorm_largeN_warps.py: rms_norm/layer_norm large-rnumel want w32, gate gives w16, -30-40%). Resolving exp: delete num_load condition, gate w32 on rnumel>THRESHOLD alone; rnumel>16384 breakpoint itself is defensible/low-impact. The tiny-rnumel w32 catastrophe (32768,256: w16=570->w32=1174us) is at rnumel=256, already excluded by >16384 (an rnumel guard, not num_load).

v4_triton_reduction_tile.change_one_line: BEFORE: if fact.num_load <= 1 and rnumel > cls.STREAM_WARPS32_MIN_ELEMS: return 32  |  AFTER: if rnumel > cls.STREAM_WARPS32_MIN_ELEMS: return 32

FIX: gate w32 on rnumel>16384 ALONE.
